Co-authored-by: vordenken <62395860+vordenken@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update SUPublicEDKey in Info.plist

* fix: make Sparkle signing step resilient — surface errors instead of silent exit

Root cause: sign_update prints errors to stdout captured by $(...),
then bash -e kills the script before they can be displayed.

- curl -fSL instead of -sL for visible HTTP errors
- Verify sign_update binary exists before running
- set +e around sign_update for graceful failure handling
- Print sign_update output on failure for diagnostics
- Signing failure is non-fatal (warns + continues unsigned)
- Pipe key via stdin (--ed-key-file -) instead of temp file

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/vordenken/ShortBlock/sessions/9f016a12-4587-4d06-b77f-86e14cb4c321

Co-authored-by: vordenken <62395860+vordenken@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace awk with sed for appcast item insertion to handle multi-line content

awk -v cannot handle literal newlines in variable values, causing
"newline in string" errors and exit code 2. Use sed's r command
to read the item block from a temp file instead.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/vordenken/ShortBlock/sessions/e7b27f3b-ac10-4363-b8cf-81ab87874bfe

Co-authored-by: vordenken <62395860+vordenken@users.noreply.github.com>

The app is signed with a local dev certificate (not notarized by Apple),
so users need to bypass Gatekeeper on first install. This adds:
- Installation steps in README Quick Start (collapsible details block)
- Same instructions as body in every GitHub Release (matching AutoPiP)

commit.message:
refactor: Partitioned.v bridge-style refactor (phases 1-6)

Phase 1: Abstract local_candidates_of as Section Variable
- Remove enumJ/filter from PartitionedSection; add
  Variable local_candidates_of : CPU -> CandidateSource
  and Hypothesis local_candidates_spec_hyp as Section variables.
- Rewrite raw_partitioned_schedule_on to call
  local_candidates_of c using the local CPU view, matching
  single_cpu_dispatch_schedule style.
- Move enumJ/filter implementation to new PartitionedEnumCandidates
  section (enum_local_candidates_of, enum_local_candidates_spec).

Phase 2: Bridge alignment confirmed
- partitioned_schedule_on_iff_local_rel now reads as: each CPU's
  cpu_schedule sched c satisfies single_cpu_dispatch_schedule spec
  (local_candidates_of c) — no unfold of local_candidates_of needed.

Phase 3: Stable public API for valid_partitioned_schedule
- Rename partitioned_schedule_on -> raw_partitioned_schedule_on
  (library-internal).
- Keep valid_partitioned_schedule as the stable external alias.
- Client-facing theorems (partitioned_schedule_implies_valid_schedule,
  local_valid_feasible_on_implies_global) now take
  valid_partitioned_schedule as premise.

Phase 4: PartitionedDispatchContext record
- Add Record PartitionedDispatchContext bundling all per-CPU invariants
  (part_assign, part_m, part_valid_assign, part_spec, part_J,
  part_candidates, part_cand_spec).
- Add partitioned_scheduler_of helper.

Phase 5: Standard global schedulability entry point
- Add partitioned_schedulable_by_on_from_local: given
  valid_partitioned_schedule + per-CPU local feasibility,
  conclude schedulable_by_on J in 3 steps.

Phase 6: FIFO compatibility confirmed
- SchedulableExamples.pair_partitioned_schedulable_by_on uses
  fifo_generic_spec + enum_local_candidates_of, confirming the
  abstract interface hosts non-EDF policies.
- partitioned_scheduler no longer takes assign or enumJ — assignment
  is encoded in the CandidateSource.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Orleans ConversationGrain and wire send-message to Ollama

- Add IConversationGrain / ConversationGrain with persistent memory state
- Add [GenerateSerializer] + [Id] to all contract types used in grain interfaces
- Configure Orleans silo with localhost clustering and memory grain storage
- Refactor CreateConversation, SendMessage, GetHistory endpoints to use grain
- Add 8,000-char max message size validation in SendMessageEndpoint
- Return clean ApiError for Ollama failures (502 BadGateway)
- Remove InMemoryConversationService from DI (file kept for reference)
- Update scaffolding-plan.md, backend-ollama-communication-plan.md, README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(tui): stabilize loading and list keys

Tighten the TUI interactions so loading states stay visually stable
and list navigation behaves consistently across screens.

This keeps search typing isolated from playback shortcuts, adds
page-style H/L movement in list screens, preserves series access on
lowercase s, and aligns the loading behavior in home, library, and
series with placeholder rows instead of loading-text flashes.

It also updates the README to match the current keybindings and Go
support, and caches empty library results so revisiting empty
libraries does not reintroduce unnecessary loading churn.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Distinguish TooManySponsoring from LowReserve in trust flag revoke

When AllowTrust/SetTrustLineFlags revokes authorization and redeems
pool-share trustlines into claimable balances, sponsorship sandwich
creation could fail because num_sponsoring is at u32::MAX. Previously
this was unconditionally mapped to LowReserve; now it correctly
propagates as OpTooManySponsoring, matching stellar-core.

Also replaces panic on non-sandwich sponsorship exhaustion with a
proper TooManySponsoring error return.

Fixes #1198

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: henyey
repository.description: A pure Rust implementation of Stellar Core

commit.message:
Fix off-by-one in offer crossing limit

Move maxOffersToCross check to BEFORE cross_offer_v10(), matching
stellar-core which checks offerTrail.size() >= maxOffersToCross before
crossing. Previously the check was after crossing, causing the exact
1000th crossing to be rejected with opEXCEEDED_WORK_LIMIT when
stellar-core allows it.

Fixes #1212

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use camelCase serialization for Cosmos DB client

CosmosClient defaults to Newtonsoft.Json which ignores System.Text.Json
[JsonPropertyName] attributes, causing 'id' to serialize as 'Id' and
triggering BadRequest 400 errors on upsert.

feat: deprecate V2, enforce V3 for all store operations

- generate/route.ts: always uses V3 agent, removed version=v2 option
- rebuild/route.ts: now supports both V3 (new) and V2 (legacy) stores
- migrate/route.ts: migrates V1 stores to V3 (was V1->V2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(tui): ctrl+s toggles mouse passthrough for terminal text selection

When mouse capture is active (default), terminals route all mouse events
to the TUI, preventing native text selection with drag.

ctrl+s now toggles 'selection mode':
- Disables mouse capture (tea.DisableMouse) so the terminal can handle
  drag-to-select natively
- Shows 'drag to select  •  ctrl+s exit select mode' in the status bar
- Pressing ctrl+s again re-enables mouse capture (tea.EnableMouseCellMotion)

Also:
- Replaces the shift+drag hint in /help with ctrl+s entry (more accurate)
- Adds ctrl+s to coreKeys (extensions cannot override)
- Updates idle status hint to show 'ctrl+s select'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement text sidecar + manifest pipeline for source document processing

- Add core text extraction pipeline for PDFs and images
  - sourceHashing: SHA-256 hashing for files, S3 objects, buffers
  - sourceExtractor: PDF text extraction with Tesseract OCR fallback
  - sidecarWriter: Atomic S3 sidecar writing (temp+rename pattern)
  - manifestService: JSONL manifest operations (load/upsert/save)
  - sourcePipeline: Batch processFolder and verifyFolder orchestration

- Add supporting utilities and tests
  - portablePaths: Cross-platform path handling
  - sourceExtraction.test.ts: Unit tests for hashing, manifest, paths

- Add CLI tool for manual processing and verification
  - scripts/process-sources.ts: process, verify, help commands

- Integrate with server
  - Hook into /api/source-upload endpoint
  - Async processing via setImmediate (non-blocking)

- Add comprehensive documentation
  - SIDECAR_IMPLEMENTATION.md: Complete technical documentation
  - IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md: High-level overview
  - SIDECAR_QUICK_REFERENCE.md: Quick reference guide

- Add OCR dependency
  - Install tesseract.js (^7.0.0) for image text extraction

Features:
  ✓ Automatic processing on source upload
  ✓ PDF text extraction with OCR fallback
  ✓ Image OCR for .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .tif, .tiff, .webp
  ✓ Atomic sidecar writing to S3
  ✓ JSONL manifest with SHA-256 integrity checks
  ✓ Manifest verification (missing, stale, mismatched detection)
  ✓ Cross-platform path handling
  ✓ Full error handling and recovery
  ✓ Unit test coverage
  ✓ Complete documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix queue eviction ghost account state

Call drop_transaction() at all 3 eviction points in try_add() that
remove from by_hash without cleaning up account_states:
- Lane evictions (pending_eviction_list)
- Expired tx cleanup
- Fee-rate eviction

Previously, evicted transactions left stale account_states entries with
state.transaction = Some(...), causing subsequent submissions from the
same account to be rejected with TryAgainLater.

Matches stellar-core's ban({txToEvict}) → dropTransaction() cleanup.

Fixes #1201

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: preserve leading zero bytes and handle all-0xFF overflow in rich-indexer prefix search upper bound (#5166)

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #5165

Problem Summary:

`get_binary_upper_boundary()` uses `BigUint::to_bytes_be()` which strips
leading zero bytes. For a 22-byte input like `00 01 4c bc ... 86 6e`,
the computed upper bound becomes `01 4c bc ... 86 6f` (21 bytes). Since
bytea comparison is lexicographic, the shorter result is far larger than
intended, causing prefix queries (`args >= $prefix AND args < $upper`)
to massively over-match — e.g. returning 103k scripts instead of 2.

~2.4% of mainnet scripts are affected (args starting with `0x00`),
spanning ACP locks, JoyID, CoTA, and others.

Additionally, when the input is all `0xFF` bytes, incrementing via
`BigUint` produces `[0x01, 0x00, ...]` which is lexicographically
*smaller* than `[0xFF, ...]` in bytea comparison (because `0x01 < 0xFF`
at byte 0), causing the prefix query to match **nothing**.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

- Replaced `BigUint` with a direct lexicographic successor computation
on the byte slice using `rposition`: find the rightmost byte that is not
`u8::MAX`, increment it, then truncate everything after it. This
produces the shortest byte string that is strictly greater than every
possible extension of the input prefix, which is exactly what the range
query needs:

```rust
if let Some(i) = value.iter().rposition(|&b| b != u8::MAX) {
    let mut result = value[..=i].to_vec();
    result[i] += 1;
    result
} else {
    // All bytes are u8::MAX — return a sentinel one byte longer
    vec![u8::MAX; value.len() + 1]
}
```

- For the all-`0xFF` overflow case, returns `vec![u8::MAX; value.len() +
1]` — a sentinel that is lexicographically greater than any extension of
the input in bytea comparison.

- Removed the now-unused `num-bigint` dependency from both
`ckb-rich-indexer` (`util/rich-indexer/Cargo.toml`) and the workspace
root (`./Cargo.toml`).

- Uses `u8::MAX` instead of `0xFF` literals throughout for clarity.

- Added 8 regression tests: real-world 22-byte args from the report,
multiple leading zeros, single zero byte, all-`0xFF` overflow sentinel,
carry propagation with truncation (e.g. `[0x00, u8::MAX, u8::MAX]` →
`[0x01]`), short prefix `0x003d`, and standard increment cases.

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- Unit test

Side effects

- None

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: quake <8990+quake@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: eval-exec <46400566+eval-exec@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Exempt fetch/control messages from global rate limiter

Fetch responses (TxSet, GeneralizedTxSet, ScpQuorumset, DontHave) and
request messages (GetTxSet, GetScpState, GetScpQuorumset) now bypass
the global flood gate rate limiter alongside SCP messages. Previously,
a single peer's flood traffic could exhaust the shared rate bucket and
starve consensus-critical control messages from other peers.

The per-peer rate limiter (PeerRateLimiter) still enforces per-peer
limits on all non-SCP traffic including fetch messages.

Fixes #1214

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add regression tests for seen-set cleanup in evict_expired, remove_applied, shift

Cover the three public methods that were missing seen-set assertions
after the AUDIT-072 fix. Each test verifies that the seen set no longer
contains a transaction hash after the method removes it from by_hash:

- test_audit_072_seen_cleared_on_evict_expired: age-based expiry
- test_audit_072_seen_cleared_on_remove_applied: post-ledger-close cleanup
- test_audit_072_seen_cleared_on_shift_autoban: pending-depth auto-ban

fix: replace triple-backtick code fences in agent prompts

Code fences (triple backticks) inside JS template literals break the TS compiler.
Replaced with EXAMPLE JSX / END EXAMPLE delimiters instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix invalid SCP envelopes creating immortal pending qset state

Move quorum-set request from before receive_scp_envelope to after it
returns Valid or Pending. Previously, forged envelopes with invalid
signatures would register pending quorum-set entries that persisted
forever, enabling memory exhaustion attacks.

Also add defense-in-depth caps:
- MAX_PENDING_QSET_REQUESTS (512) limits total pending entries
- MAX_PENDING_NODE_IDS (64) limits node IDs per pending entry

Matches stellar-core which verifies envelope signatures before any
quorum-set fetch logic (HerderImpl.cpp:876-893).

Fixes #1205

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove upgrade-insecure-requests from CSP — blank homepage on HTTP deployments (#525)

* Initial plan

* fix: remove upgrade-insecure-requests from CSP to fix blank homepage over HTTP

The upgrade-insecure-requests CSP directive instructs browsers to
upgrade same-host HTTP requests to HTTPS (preserving port). In the
default plain-HTTP Docker deployment (port 4174), the browser upgrades
every asset URL to https://host:4174/... and sends a TLS Client Hello
to the HTTP nginx port. nginx cannot parse TLS bytes as HTTP and returns
400 with no method/URI (the observed "400 - -" log pattern). All
JS/CSS bundles fail to load, React never mounts, page stays blank.

Fix: remove "; upgrade-insecure-requests" from the CSP string.
This directive is intended for HTTPS-only sites and is harmful on
plain-HTTP servers. Removing it does not weaken security for HTTP
deployments.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/DanielVolz/medassist-ng/sessions/9c4db7bd-1272-49ca-abf3-73c2ad5a5354
